questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The coefficient of $x^{20}$ in the expansion of $\left(1+3 x+3 x^2+x^3\right)^{20}$ is
questions.solution:
Given expansion is $\left(1+3 x+3 x^2+x^3\right)^{20}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\left((1+x)^3\right)^{20} \\
& =(1+x)^{60}
\end{aligned}
$$
In the expansion of $(a+b)^n,(r+1)$ th term is given by
$$
t_{r+1}={ }^n C_r a^r \cdot b^{n-r}
$$
or $\quad t_{r+1}={ }^n C_r a^{n-r} \cdot b^r$
$\therefore 21$ st term in Eq. (i) will contain the coefficient of $x^{20}$ and it is given by
$$
t_{21}={ }^{60} C_{20} x^{20}, 1^{40}={ }^{60} C_{20} x^{20}
$$
$\therefore$ Coefficient of $x^{20}$ in $(1+x)^{60}$ is ${ }^{60} C_{20}$ or ${ }^{60} C_{40}$.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The term independent of $x$ in $\left[\sqrt{\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)}+\sqrt{\left(\frac{3}{2 x^{2}}\right)}\right]^{10}$ is
questions.solution:
Given, expansion is $\left[\sqrt{\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)}+\sqrt{\left(\frac{3}{2 x^{2}}\right)}\right]^{10}$
Let $t_{r+1}$ be general term, then
$$
\begin{aligned}
t_{r+1} &={ }^{10} C_{r}\left(\sqrt{\frac{x}{3}}\right)^{10-r}\left(\sqrt{\frac{3}{2 x^{2}}}\right)^{r} \\
&={ }^{10} C_{r} \frac{x \frac{10-r}{2}}{\frac{10-r}{2}} \cdot \frac{3^{i / 2}}{2^{r / 2} \cdot x^{r}}={ }^{10} C_{r} x-\frac{r}{2}-r \cdot \frac{3^{r-5}}{2^{r / 2}}
\end{aligned}
$$
For the term independent of $x$.
Puts $5-\frac{r}{2}-r=0$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad 5-\frac{3}{2} r=0 \Rightarrow \frac{3}{2} r=5 \Rightarrow r=\frac{10}{3}
$$
Fractional value of $r$ is not possible. So, no term is independent of $x$.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
In the expansion of $\left(x^{3}-\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{n}$ where n is a positive integer, the sum of the coefficients of $\mathrm{x}^{5}$ and $\mathrm{x}^{10}$ is $0 .$
What is the sum of the coefficients of the two middle terms?
questions.solution:
$\left(x^{3}-\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{n}$
$\quad$ General term, $T_{r+1}={ }^{n} C_{r}\left(x^{3}\right)^{n-r} \cdot\left(-\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right)^{r}$
$\quad={ }^{n} C_{r} \cdot x^{(3 n-3 r)} \cdot(-1)^{r} \cdot x^{-2 r}$
$={ }^{n} C_{r} \cdot(-1)^{r} \cdot x^{(3 n-5 r)}$ ...(i)
For the coefficient $\mathrm{x}^{5}$
Put $3 n-5 r=5$
$5 r=3 n-5$
$\therefore \mathrm{r}=\frac{3 \mathrm{n}}{5}-1$
$\therefore$ Coefficient of $x^{5}={ }^{n} C\left(\frac{3 n}{5}-1\right)^{(-1)}\left(\frac{3 n}{5}-1\right)$
For the coefficient of $\mathrm{x}^{10}$
Put $3 n-5 r=10$
$5 r=3 n-10$
$\therefore \mathrm{r}=\frac{3 \mathrm{n}}{5}-2$ 
$\therefore$ Coefficient of $x^{10}={ }^{n} C_{\left(\frac{3 n}{5}-2\right)^{(-1)}}\left(\frac{3 n}{5}-2\right)$
The sum of the coefficient of $x^{5}$ and $x^{10}=0$
$\Rightarrow{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{n}}{5}-1\right)^{(-1)}\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{n}}{5}-1\right)}+{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{n}}{5}-2\right)}(-1)^{\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{n}}{5}-2\right)}=0$
$\Rightarrow$
$\left.(-1)^{\frac{3 n}{5}}\left[{ }^{n} C_{\left(\frac{3 n}{5}-1\right)} \cdot(-1)^{-1}+{ }^{n} C_{\left(\frac{3 n}{5}-2\right.}\right) \cdot(-1)^{(-2)}\right]=0$
$\Rightarrow-{ }^{n} C_{\left(\frac{3 n}{5}-1\right)}+{ }^{n} C_{\left(\frac{3 n}{5}-2\right)}=0$ ...(ii)

$\mathrm{n}=15$
Total term in the expansion of $\left(\mathrm{x}^{3}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}}\right)^{15}$ is 16 . $\therefore$ middle term $=8^{\text {th }}$ term and $9^{\text {th }}$ term $\mathrm{T}_{8}=\mathrm{T}_{(7+1)}={ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{7} \cdot(-1)^{7} \cdot \mathrm{x}^{(3 \times 15-5 \times 7)}$
$=-{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{7} \cdot \mathrm{x}^{10} \quad$ (from eq. (i))
$\mathrm{T}_{9}=\mathrm{T}_{(8+1)}={ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{8} \cdot(-1)^{8} \cdot \mathrm{x}^{(3 \times 15-5 \times 8)}$
$=-{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{8} \cdot \mathrm{x}^{5} \quad$ (from eq. (ii))
The sum of the coefficients of the two middle terms
$=-{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{7}+{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{8}=-{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{7}+{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{7} \cdot\left[\because{ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}={ }^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{n}-\mathrm{r}}\right]$
$=0$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the middle term in the expansion of $(1+x)^{2 n}$ is the greatest term, then $x$ lies in the interval
questions.solution:
In the expansion of $(1+x)^{2 n}$, middle term is ${ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_n x^n$. Since, middle term is the greatest term.
$\therefore{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_n x^n>{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_{n-1} x^{n-1}$
$\text { and }{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_r x^n>{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_{n+1} x^{n+1}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad x>\frac{{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_{n-1}}{{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_n} \text { and } x < \frac{{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_n}{{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_{n+1}} \\
& \text { Hence, } x \in\left(\frac{{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_n}{{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_{n+1}}, \frac{{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_n}{{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_{n+1}}\right)
\end{aligned}$
Hence, $x \in\left(\frac{{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_n}{{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_{n+1}}, \frac{{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_n}{{ }^{2 n} \mathrm{C}_{n+1}}\right)$
$\begin{aligned}
& x \in\left(\begin{array}{c}
\frac{(2 n) !}{(n-1) !(2 n-n+1)} \times \frac{n !(2 n-n) !}{(2 n) !} \\
\frac{(2 n) !}{n !(2 n-n) !} \times \frac{(n-1) !(2 n-n+1)}{(2 n) !}
\end{array}\right) \\
& x \in\left(\frac{n(n-1) ! n !}{n(n-1) !(n+1) n !}, \frac{(n+1) n !(n-1) !}{n(n-1) ! n !}\right) \\
& \therefore \quad x \in\left(\frac{n}{n+1}, \frac{n+1}{n}\right)
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: In the expansion of $\left(1+3 x+3 x^2+x^3\right)^{2 n}$, the term which has greatest binomial coefficient, is
questions.solution:
$\because$ Middle term has greatest binomial coefficient.
In the expansion of $\left(1+3 x+3 x^2+x^3\right)^{2 n}$
$=\left((1+x)^3\right)^{2 n}=(1+x)^{6 n}$
$\because 6 n$ is even
So, middle term of $(1+x)^{6 n}=T\left(\frac{6 n}{2}+1\right)$ $=T_{(3 n+1)}=(3 n+1)$ th term.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: If in the expansion of <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow></msup><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo></math> the coefficients of <math><mi>x</mi></math> and <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> are <math><mn>3</mn></math> and <math><mo>–</mo><mn>6</mn></math> respectively, then the value of <math><mi>m</mi></math> is <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>N</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>
questions.solution:
Given expansion =1+mC1x+mC2x2+....1-nC1x+nC2x2-....
Coefficient of x2=-nC1+mC1=m-n=3
Coefficient of x2=nC2-mC1mC1+mC2=nn-12-mn+mm-12
⇒ nn-12-mn+mm-12=-6
n=m–3
⇒ m-3m-42+mm-12-mm-3=-6
⇒m2-7m+12+m2-m-2m2+6m=-12
⇒-2m=-24⇒m=12
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the coefficient of $x^7$ in $\left[a x^2+\left(\frac{1}{b x}\right)\right]^{11}$ equals the coefficient of $x^{-7}$ in $\left[a x^2-\left(\frac{1}{b x}\right)\right]^{11}$, then $a$ and $b$ satisfy the relation
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& T_{r+1} \text { in the expansion }\left[a x^2+\frac{1}{b x}\right]^{11}={ }^{11} C_r\left(a x^2\right)^{11-r}\left(\frac{1}{b x}\right)^r \\
& ={ }^{11} C_r(a)^{11-r}(b)^{-r}(x)^{22-2 r-r} \\
& \Rightarrow 22-3 r=7 \Rightarrow r=5 \\
& \therefore \text { coefficient of } x^7={ }^{11} C_5(a)^6(b)^{-5} ....(i)
\end{aligned}
$$
Again $T_{r+1}$ in the expansion $\left[a x-\frac{1}{b x^2}\right]^{11}={ }^{11} C_r(a x)^{11-r}\left(-\frac{1}{b x^2}\right)^r$ $={ }^{11} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{a}^{11-\mathrm{r}}(-1)^{\mathrm{r}} \times(\mathrm{b})^{-\mathrm{r}}(\mathrm{x})^{-2 \mathrm{r}}(\mathrm{x})^{11-\mathrm{r}}$
Now $11-3 r=-7 \Rightarrow 3 r=18 \Rightarrow r=6$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \text { coefficient of } x^{-7}={ }^{11} C_6 a^5 \times 1 \times(b)-6 \\
& \Rightarrow{ }^{11} C_5(a)^6(b)^{-5}={ }^{11} C_6 a^5 \times(b)^{-6} \\
& \Rightarrow a b=1
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: If sum of coefficients in the expression of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>35</mn></msup></math> is equal to sum of coefficients in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>α</mi><mi>y</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>35</mn></msup><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Let

S1=αx2-2x+135

S2=x-αy35

Put x=y=1. Then, we get

S1=α-135

⇒S1=C035α35-10+C135α34-12+....+C3535-135

And,

S2=1-α35=-α-135

⇒2α-135=0

⇒α=1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: Let $n=1 !+4 !+7 !+\ldots+400 !$. Then ten's digit of $n$ is
questions.solution:
Given, $n=1 !+4 !+7 !+\ldots+400 !$ $1 !=1,4 !=24,7 !=5040,10 !=3628800$ and further the terms has last two digits $=00$
So,
$1 !+4 !+7 !+\ldots+400 !=\ldots \ldots 65$
Here ten digit is 6 .
Hence, 6 is the answer.

=322-136C6932+C79·2·-13=1754

questions.q:
Consider the following statements. For any integer $\mathrm{n}$,
I. $n^{2}+3$ is never divisible by 17 .
II. $n^{2}+4$ is never divisible by 17 .
Then
options: both I and II are true
both I and II are fal se
I is false and II is true
I is true and II is fal se
questions.solution: $n^{2}+4$ is divisible by 17 check at $n=9$ $\because \frac{n^{2}+3}{17}=\frac{n^{2}+4}{17}-\frac{1}{17}$ not divisible by 17
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The remainder when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mn>2021</mn></mfenced><mn>2023</mn></msup></math> is divided by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math> is
questions.solution:
The remainder when 2021 divided by 7 is -2. Hence, the problem reduces to finding the remainder when -22023 is divided by 7.

=-22022×-27

=-2×220227

=-2×236747

=-2×86747

=-2×1+76747

Using binomial theorem, we get

=-2×1+7k7 where k is an integer.

=-2-14k7

Clearly, the remainder when -14k is divisible by 7 is 0.

=-27

=-2-5+57=57

Hence, the remainder is 5.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> is the degree of the polynomial, <math><msup><mrow><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt><mo>-</mo><msqrt><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mrow><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> is the coefficient of <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>n</mi></mrow></msup></math> in it, then the ordered pair <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>m</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
25x3+1-5x3-18+ 25x3+1+5x3-18

Rationalizing denominators of both the brackets,

=5x3+1+ 5x3-1  8+ 5x3+1 - 5x3-1  8

=28C0 5x3+18+8C25x3+165x3-12+8C45x3+145x3-14+8C65x3+125x3-16+8C85x3-18

=25x3-14+285x3+135x3-1+705x3+125x3-12+285x3+15x3-13+5x3+14

So, n=12 and 

m=254+140.53+70.54+140.53+54=160000=204
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The coefficient of $x^3 y^4 z^5$ in the expansion of $(x y+y z+x z)^6$ is
questions.solution:
We have
$\begin{aligned}
(x y+y z+z x)^6 & =\sum_{r+s+t=6} \frac{6 !}{r ! s ! t !}(x y)^r(y z)^s(z x)^t \\
& =\sum_{r+s+t=6} \frac{6 !}{r ! s ! t !} x^{r+t} y^{r+s} z^{s+t}
\end{aligned}$
If the general term in the above expansion. contains $x^3 y^4 z^5$, then
$r+t=3, r+s=4 \text { and } s+t=5$
Also, $r+s+t=6$
Solving these equations, we get
$r=1, s=3, t=2$
$\therefore$ Coefficient of
$x^3 y^4 z^5=\frac{6 !}{1 ! 3 ! 2 !}=\frac{6 !}{2 ! 3 !}=60$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The coefficient of <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>9</mn></mrow></msup></math> in the expansion of <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi> </mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mo>…</mo><mo>…</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>100</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></math> is
questions.solution:
1+x 1+x2….(1+x100)
Possible combination of 9 are (0, 9), (1, 8), (2, 7), (3, 6), (4, 5), (1, 2, 6), (1, 3, 5), (2, 3, 4)
So, coefficient = 8.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Coefficient of <math><msup><mrow><mi>α</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow></msup></math> in the expansion of <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>p</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>p</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>q</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>p</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>q</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>q</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo></math> where <math><mi>α</mi><mo>≠</mo><mo>-</mo><mi>q</mi></math> and <math><mi>p</mi><mo>≠</mo><mi>q</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
The terms are in GP with a=α+pm-1,r=α+qα+p and number of terms =m.
S=α+pm−1α+qα+pm−1α+qα+p−1=α+qm-α+pmq−p
Coefficient of αt in 1q-pα+qm-α+pm
=1q-pCtm qm-t-Ctm pm-t
=Ctpm-t-qm-tmp-q
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
